summary: A stellar Spring‑'90 Copps show highlighted by a scorching Scarlet > Fire, captured on a soundboard that was initially rough but has been fixed and now sounds good.
review: The performance is widely praised – the second set’s Scarlet > Fire transition, Brent’s heartfelt Easy To Love You, and several rare moments (Believe It Or Not, Hey Jude) make this one of the best shows of the early‑90 tour. The original SBD was described as “horrible” with low vocals, but a later remaster corrected channel inversion and now delivers solid sound quality that most listeners find acceptable. Overall the musical excitement far outweighs the recording’s quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and rave about the music; recording issues are noted but have been remedied, yielding a solid combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Initial SBD was described as muddy with faint vocals; later fixes (channel inversion correction) and remastering improved clarity. Some note that a version on nugs.net sounds better, but the current file is considered an above‑average audience‑like soundboard.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is strong with tight jams, while the second set erupts on Scarlet > Fire and a high‑energy finish; transitions are praised for their excitement and interplay.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work remains powerful; some reviewers note his vocals are faint in the original SBD but still recognizable.
band_member_comments.Bob: His interplay with Jerry during Scarlet > Fire is highlighted as a key moment of the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s playing is erratic – brilliant on Easy To Love You and Hey Jude for some, but lyrics were garbled and he sounded “fucked up” on other numbers.
